name: Everyday Application: Electricity, Magnetism, Light & Sound

sourceRefs.label: tnpscguru.in - Electricity & Magnetism, Light, Sound (everyday applications) Q&A series

researchNotes: OCR search for 'electricity', 'magnet', 'electromagnetic' etc. returned hits, but all were false positives belonging to OTHER syllabus clauses bundled in the same sentence as this one (nuclear physics, chemistry, industrial-policy GS) — saved to tools/aptitude-evidence-stash.md for their correct future topics rather than misused here. This topic itself rests on web research. Raw pool was 26 items across electricity/magnetism (8), light (12), sound (6) — trimmed to the clearest, most syllabus-relevant ~16 reading items and ~12 mock items, balanced across all four sub-areas rather than exhausting any one. One sourced fact (a 'Fleming right-hand rule' attribution for the magnetic field around a current-carrying wire) is flagged below — the standard name for that specific rule is the Right-Hand Thumb Rule; Fleming's rules are conventionally for motors (left-hand) and generators (right-hand), so this looks like loose terminology on the source's part, presented with the correction alongside it.

reading.intro: This single syllabus clause bundles four physics areas at once. Real coaching-site evidence shows TNPSC tests a mix of 'why does X happen in daily life' (sky's blue colour, bulb filament shape) and direct formula/definition recall (Ohm's law, lens power, speed of sound) — cover both styles.

qa.question: What is the resistance of a wire after it's stretched to exactly double its original length (volume unchanged)?
qa.answer: It becomes 4 times the original resistance. Since R = ρL/A and volume (A×L) stays constant, doubling L halves A — so R_new = ρ(2L)/(A/2) = 4×(ρL/A) = 4R. A 1-ohm wire becomes a 4-ohm wire.

qa.question: Which of these equations are correctly stated? (i) H=V²It (ii) V=IR (iii) P=VI (iv) F=mV²
qa.answer: (ii) V=IR (Ohm's Law) and (iii) P=VI (electrical power) are correct. (i) is wrong (the correct heating-effect formula is H=I²Rt, Joule's law) and (iv) is wrong (force is F=ma, not mV²).

qa.question: Why is soft iron used for magnetic shielding?
qa.answer: Soft iron is highly magnetically permeable — it draws in (concentrates) external magnetic field lines into itself, keeping the shielded region inside relatively field-free.

qa.question: Why is a light bulb's filament made as a 'coiled coil' rather than a straight wire?
qa.answer: A coiled-coil shape packs a long filament into a small space and reduces heat loss through convection currents inside the bulb, so the filament reaches a higher, more efficient glowing temperature.

qa.question: What rule gives the direction of the magnetic field produced by a current-carrying conductor?
qa.answer: Commonly called the Right-Hand (Thumb) Rule. One sourced TNPSC-prep answer calls this 'Fleming's right-hand rule' — be aware that, strictly, Fleming's right-hand rule is normally taught for generators/induced EMF, and Fleming's left-hand rule for motor force; the field-direction-around-a-wire rule is conventionally just the Right-Hand Thumb Rule. If this exact MCQ appears, the coaching-site convention expects 'Fleming right-hand rule' as the labelled answer — know both names.

qa.question: Why is white NOT considered a 'prime' (primary) colour?
qa.answer: White light is a mixture of all the primary colours (commonly Red, Green, Blue in the additive light model) — it's a combination, not a primary colour itself.

qa.question: A lens has power +5D (dioptres). What is its focal length, and what does the positive sign tell you?
qa.answer: Focal length = 1/Power = 1/5 m = 0.2 m = 20 cm. The positive sign indicates it's a convex (converging) lens.

qa.question: What does an achromatic lens do?
qa.answer: It corrects chromatic aberration, producing a clear image unaffected by the colour-fringing that a simple lens would otherwise introduce.

qa.question: Why is the sky blue?
qa.answer: Scattering — specifically Rayleigh scattering, where shorter (blue) wavelengths of sunlight scatter more strongly off air molecules than longer (red) wavelengths, spreading blue light across the sky.

qa.question: In which medium (among vacuum, air, glass, water) is the speed of light slowest?
qa.answer: Glass — light slows down more in denser/more optically refractive media, and of these four, glass has the highest refractive index.

qa.question: Convert a wavelength of 7000 Å (angstroms) to nanometres.
qa.answer: 1 Å = 0.1 nm, so 7000 Å = 700 nm. (This corresponds to red light, near the edge of the visible spectrum.)

qa.question: The Raman Effect (discovered by C.V. Raman) is the scattering of light by which state of matter?
qa.answer: Pure liquids — Raman's original Nobel Prize-winning work studied light scattering in liquids.

qa.question: Who invented the phonograph, and what did it do?
qa.answer: Thomas Alva Edison invented the phonograph, a device that played back recorded sound.

qa.question: What is the formula relating the speed of sound to frequency and wavelength?
qa.answer: v = nλ (or v = fλ) — speed equals frequency (n or f) multiplied by wavelength (λ).

qa.question: How is the 'speed of sound' defined in terms of distance and time?
qa.answer: It's the distance travelled by a sound wave in 1 second (i.e., distance/time with time fixed at 1 second), conventionally denoted 'v'.

questions.prompt: A wire of resistance 2 ohms is stretched to exactly double its length (volume unchanged). What is its new resistance?
questions.explanation: Resistance scales by 4× when length doubles at constant volume (R=ρL/A, and A halves when L doubles at fixed volume): new R = 4 × R_old = 4 × 2 = 8 ohms.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is the correctly stated form of Ohm's Law?
questions.explanation: V = IR is Ohm's Law. P=VI is a valid power formula too but isn't Ohm's Law itself; the other two are wrong as written.

questions.prompt: Soft iron is preferred for magnetic shielding because it:
questions.explanation: It draws field lines into itself, shielding the enclosed region.

questions.prompt: A light bulb's filament is made as a 'coiled coil' mainly to:
options: Reduce heat loss by convection and save space
questions.explanation: Compact shape, reduced convective heat loss, higher operating temperature.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is NOT considered a primary (prime) colour of light?
questions.explanation: White is a mixture of the primary colours, not a primary colour itself.

questions.prompt: A lens has a power of +5 dioptres. Its focal length is:
An achromatic lens is designed to:
options: Correct chromatic aberration for a clear image

questions.prompt: The blue colour of the sky is caused by:
questions.explanation: Rayleigh scattering of shorter wavelengths by the atmosphere.

questions.prompt: Among vacuum, air, glass, and water, the speed of light is slowest in:
questions.explanation: Glass has the highest refractive index of the four listed, so light travels slowest through it.

questions.prompt: 7000 Å (angstroms) is equal to how many nanometres?
The Raman Effect refers to the scattering of light by:
questions.explanation: C.V. Raman's discovery concerned scattering in pure liquids.
